100 Main St, Montpelier, VT 05602.notes: The rear facade of this 3-story red clapboard building is visible; upper two floors show regularly spaced double-hung windows (~6-7 windows per floor), while the ground floor has fewer and smaller openings partially obscured by a canopy overhang, utility equipment, and parked vehicles.

112 State St, Montpelier, VT 05602.notes: The rear brick facade is largely visible showing four floors with regularly spaced double-hung windows and arched ground-floor openings, though partial obstruction from trees and parked cars limits precise ground-floor assessment.

27 Langdon St, Montpelier, VT 05602.source: missing — no back photo or Street View screenshot

40 Main St, Montpelier, VT 05602.notes: The rear brick facade shows two upper floors with approximately 8 regularly spaced double-hung windows each, but the ground floor is almost entirely obscured by floodwater and a lower attached structure/lean-to roof, leaving only a small blue door visible at ground level.

54 Elm St, Montpelier, VT 05602.notes: The rear brick facade shows approximately 3 stories with several window openings visible on upper floors, but the ground floor is heavily obscured by propane tanks, graffiti-covered surfaces, and the stop sign structure, making lower-level fenestration difficult to assess; balconies and porch structures were excluded from the estimate.
